title: Great Tree Morning
desc: Before the scholars and pilgrims arrive, the lower root court of the Great Tree needs its basins and light cups set for dawn.

steps.text:
At first light, Runic Grove feels less like a forest than a conversation that started before anyone now living was invited. The Great Tree rises above the rest of the grove in impossible patience, its bark crossed with countless runes so old even the most stubborn scholars have had to settle for reverence where certainty failed them. Dew gathers in the seams of those carvings. Root arches lift from the soil like walls deciding not to finish becoming architecture. When the sun reaches the upper branches, the rune cuts catch it in pale gold threads that move slowly down the trunk as the morning grows.

The root court attendant is already at work among the lower basins and hanging cups. "Polish the dew basins if you want the ground line," she says. "Or reset the little light cups beneath the root arches if you prefer the hanging work. Once the visitors arrive, they will call this place beautiful. Before that, somebody has to make sure it can be seen properly."

options.label: Polish the dew basins around the Great Tree roots
Reset the hanging light cups beneath the root arches

steps.text:
The dew basins are set where the great roots divide, shallow stone bowls meant to catch the morning runoff before it slips back into the soil. Overnight leaf dust and drifting pollen have dulled their surfaces. You wipe each basin clean, skim the collected water, and set them back so they mirror the trunk and roots without haze. As the light strengthens, the reflections sharpen. Rune-marked bark appears again in each bowl, doubled and trembling slightly with every new drop of dew.

By the time you finish the last basin, the whole root court looks deeper than the ground should allow. Above you the runes glow faintly in the moving dawn. Below, they shimmer in the water as if the Tree had decided one version of itself was not enough. The attendant watches the basins wake one by one and nods. "Better. A place this old should never look half awake."

options.label: Set the cloth aside and leave the basins holding dawn

steps.text:
The little light cups hang on braided cords under the lower root arches, where they catch and soften the first sunlight before it reaches the central court. Two cups have drifted crooked in the night breeze and another has gathered enough grit to scatter light instead of carrying it. You clean the cups, retie the cords, and draw the line back into even spacing so the morning radiance passes under the roots in a clear warm sequence.

When the sun reaches them, the cups answer beautifully. Golden light blooms beneath the roots, then spills across the court stones in long gentle bars that make the runes look carved from brightness rather than bark. The attendant braces one hand on a root and studies the change with the calm of someone who has seen this every day and still respects it. "Good," she says. "Now the Tree can introduce itself properly."

options.label: Coil the spare cord and leave the root arches lit
